title: Replace raw HTML form controls with shadcn components and fix hardcoded color tokens
description: Systematic upgrade of raw <select> and <textarea> elements to shadcn/ui equivalents, and replacement of hardcoded Tailwind color values (red-*, gray-*) with semantic design tokens (destructive, border) for consistency and theme-correctness across the frontend.

userStories.title: Create shadcn Textarea UI component
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable shadcn-style Textarea component at src/components/ui/textarea.jsx so other stories can import it. Model it after the existing Input component — use cn() for className merging, forward all props, export as named export.
acceptanceCriteria: File src/components/ui/textarea.jsx exists and exports a named Textarea function
Textarea accepts className, disabled, and all standard textarea HTML props via ...props spread

Default className applies: flex min-h-[80px] w-full rounded-md border border-input bg-transparent px-3 py-2 text-sm shadow-sm transition-colors placeholder:text-muted-foreground focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50
Uses cn() from @/lib/utils for className merging
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: No radix-ui primitive needed — just a styled wrapper around a plain <textarea>. Keep under 40 lines.

userStories.title: Replace raw <select> in ContactForm with shadcn Select
userStories.description: As a user, the relationship dropdown in the contact form should use the shadcn Select component for consistent styling and accessibility, replacing the raw <select> element and removing the SELECT_CLS constant.
acceptanceCriteria: Raw <select> at line ~62 in src/components/ContactForm.jsx replaced with Select/SelectTrigger/SelectContent/SelectItem from @/components/ui/select
SELECT_CLS constant removed from ContactForm.jsx
All RELATIONSHIP_OPTIONS still render as SelectItem elements
Selected value still controlled via form.relationship state
onChange still calls handleChange or equivalent to update form state
disabled prop still propagated to SelectTrigger
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: shadcn Select uses onValueChange (not onChange). The value prop goes on Select root. Pass disabled to SelectTrigger. Import from src/components/ui/select.jsx using individual named exports, not a barrel.

userStories.title: Replace raw <select> in FeedbackWidget with shadcn Select
userStories.description: As a user, the category dropdown in the feedback widget should use the shadcn Select component, replacing the raw <select> element and removing the SELECT_CLS constant.
acceptanceCriteria: Raw <select> at line ~120 in src/components/FeedbackWidget.jsx replaced with Select/SelectTrigger/SelectContent/SelectItem
SELECT_CLS constant removed from FeedbackWidget.jsx
All category options still render as SelectItem elements with correct values
Selected value still controlled via form.category state
onChange calls still update the form state correctly
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Use onValueChange on the Select root to update state. Keep consistent with US-502 pattern.

userStories.title: Replace raw <textarea> in FeedbackWidget with shadcn Textarea
userStories.description: As a user, the message textarea in the feedback widget should use the shadcn Textarea component, replacing the raw <textarea> element and removing the TEXTAREA_CLS constant.
acceptanceCriteria: Raw <textarea> at line ~127 in src/components/FeedbackWidget.jsx replaced with Textarea from @/components/ui/textarea
TEXTAREA_CLS constant removed from FeedbackWidget.jsx
value and onChange props still bound to form.message state
placeholder still preserved
rows/resize behavior preserved via className if needed
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Textarea component must exist (US-501 creates it). Import from src/components/ui/textarea.jsx.

userStories.title: Replace raw <textarea> in NewEventFormFields with shadcn Textarea
userStories.description: As a user, the notes textarea in the new event form should use the shadcn Textarea component, replacing the raw <textarea> element and removing the TEXTAREA_CLASSES constant.
acceptanceCriteria: Raw <textarea> at line ~41 in src/components/NewEventFormFields.jsx replaced with Textarea from @/components/ui/textarea
TEXTAREA_CLASSES constant removed from NewEventFormFields.jsx
value bound to notes state, onChange still calls setNotes
id='event-notes' preserved for label association
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Textarea component must exist (US-501 creates it). The TEXTAREA_CLASSES string duplicates the Textarea component's default styles — just delete it.

userStories.title: Replace raw <select> and <textarea> in OfferNegotiationPanel with shadcn components
userStories.description: As a user, the offer selector, template selector, and notes textarea in the offer negotiation panel should use shadcn Select and Textarea components, replacing raw elements and removing SELECT_CLS.
acceptanceCriteria: Raw <select> at line ~64 in src/components/OfferNegotiationPanel.jsx replaced with Select/SelectTrigger/SelectContent/SelectItem
Raw <select> at line ~80 replaced with shadcn Select
Raw <textarea> at line ~110 replaced with Textarea from @/components/ui/textarea
SELECT_CLS constant removed from OfferNegotiationPanel.jsx
All controlled state (selectedOfferId, templateKey, customScript) still correctly updated
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Two selects and one textarea. Use onValueChange for selects. This file may be close to 300-line limit — check and split if needed.

userStories.title: Fix SkeletonCalendarCell hardcoded border colors to use design token
userStories.description: As a developer, the skeleton calendar cell should use the semantic border-border token instead of hardcoded gray values for theme consistency.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/components/SkeletonCalendarCell.jsx, border-gray-100 and dark:border-gray-700 replaced with border-border
No other hardcoded gray or color classes remain in the file
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Single one-line change. The border-border token handles light/dark automatically.

userStories.title: Fix NotificationBell badge to use semantic destructive token
userStories.description: As a developer, the notification count badge should use bg-destructive and text-destructive-foreground tokens instead of hardcoded bg-red-500 text-white for theme consistency.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/components/NotificationBell.jsx, bg-red-500 replaced with bg-destructive
text-white on the badge replaced with text-destructive-foreground
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Single targeted change on the badge span around line 103. text-destructive-foreground ensures contrast in both themes.

userStories.title: Fix ApiErrorMessage hardcoded red colors to use semantic destructive tokens
userStories.description: As a developer, ApiErrorMessage should use semantic destructive tokens instead of hardcoded Tailwind red- classes so error states look correct in both light and dark themes.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/components/ApiErrorMessage.jsx, border-red-200 replaced with border-destructive/30
bg-red-50 replaced with bg-destructive/5
text-red-500 replaced with text-destructive
text-red-800 replaced with text-destructive
border-red-300 replaced with border-destructive/40
text-red-700 replaced with text-destructive
hover:bg-red-100 replaced with hover:bg-destructive/10
hover:text-red-700 replaced with hover:text-destructive
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: All red- classes in this file are purely for error state styling — they should all map to destructive tokens. Use /opacity suffixes for backgrounds to avoid covering the card background.
